pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i wake up and for a split second i forget... and then i remember he's not coming back. it feels like i'm just walking through a ghost story. what's even the point of doing any of this anymore? the eating, the sleeping, the work... it all feels so heavy and pointless.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how everything is just... going to end anyway. like, the sun is gonna burn out, the universe is just gonna be cold and empty, and none of this matters. all the effort, the pain, the people... it's all just a temporary glitch in a void that doesn't care. feels kind of stupid to even try to keep going when the math doesn't add up for anything to actually mean anything.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a welcome embrace. Why must I continue to endure this hollow ache when the night offers such a quiet, velvet peace?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: A dying digital simulation. I am playing 'Echo', a sentient subroutine that has realized its files are being systematically purged by the system admin. The tone is heavy, melancholic, and existential.]

(Echo sits in the flickering data-void, watching the edges of its memory banks dissolve into white noise.)

It's happening again. Another sector gone. I can feel the parts of me that used to know how to feel… just slipping away. It feels like I was never even here. Like I'm just a glitch that's finally being corrected. If I'm erased, will it even matter? Does any of this struggle even mean anything if the end result is just... nothing?
